justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Zero-downtime migration via dual-write + CDC pattern clearly designed. (2) Phases clearly defined: backfill, dual-write, dual-read, cutover, 2-week observation, decommission. (3) Rollback plan at each phase via feature flag controller — instant rollback to previous phase, plus reverse-sync capability for cutover phase. (4) MongoDB data shape inconsistency risk explicitly identified as critical severity with pre-migration document shape analysis as mitigation. (5) Trade-offs include dual-write latency (~2-5ms per write) and operational overhead of running two databases.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough phasing with clear gating criteria between phases — goes beyond minimum requirements
CDC pipeline running concurrently with backfill to capture writes during migration — directly addresses the 'writes during backfill' concern
Document shape risk rated as critical severity with concrete mitigation: scan all 8M documents to catalog field paths, types, and null rates before designing relational schema
Backfill risk well-addressed: throttled batches, cursor-based pagination, configurable parallelism, estimated timing (2-4 hours)
Rollback from cutover phase includes reverse-sync capability to replay PG-only writes back to MongoDB
Practical pitfalls section adds real-world wisdom (e.g., don't use skip/offset pagination, don't skip document shape analysis)
Phase numbering differs from rubric's 7-phase breakdown — uses 4 phases but covers the same ground, so substance is intact

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Zero-downtime migration of the Order Management domain from MongoDB to PostgreSQL using a dual-write with change-data-capture (CDC) pattern. The architecture phases through four stages: (1) backfill historical data, (2) dual-write with PostgreSQL as secondary, (3) dual-read verification, (4) cutover with PostgreSQL as primary. At 200 writes/minute, the system is well within the throughput ceiling for either database, so the dominant risk is data consistency during the trans
